edges.description: Loss of sialidase activity prevents desialylation, so sialylated substrates accumulate.

meta.association: Biallelic NEU1 variants abolishing lysosomal neuraminidase-1

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis: A Review of Morphology and Molecular Biology of a Rare Pediatric Disorder.
evidence.snippet:
caused by α-N-acetyl neuraminidase deficiency resulting from a mutation in
the neuraminidase gene (NEU1)

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis type II: Expansion of phenotypic spectrum and identification of a common mutation in seven patients.
evidence.snippet: All seven phenotypically heterogeneous patients had the same pathogenic variant (c.679G > A; p.Gly227Arg) at a homozygous level in the NEU1 gene

nodes.description: Biallelic NEU1 variants abolish lysosomal neuraminidase-1 (alpha-N-acetyl neuraminidase), the sialidase that removes terminal sialic acid from sialoglycoconjugates. NEU1 normally acts in a complex with the protective protein/cathepsin A.

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis: A Review of Morphology and Molecular Biology of a Rare Pediatric Disorder.
evidence.snippet:
caused by α-N-acetyl neuraminidase deficiency resulting from a mutation in
the neuraminidase gene (NEU1)

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis, Emerging therapeutic targets and Treatment in Sialidosis.
evidence.snippet: caused by mutations in the NEU1 gene, encoding the lysosomal sialidase NEU1

nodes.description: Without sialidase activity, sialylated oligosaccharides and glycopeptides accumulate in lysosomes (and are excreted in urine), producing multisystem storage disease.

evidence.reference_title: Sialidosis: A Review of Morphology and Molecular Biology of a Rare Pediatric Disorder.
